message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles, Zap, Heart } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Bizarre Lineage Crazy Diamond: Complete Build & Moveset Guide 2026",
  description: "Master the Bizarre Lineage Crazy Diamond stand with our 2026 guide. Learn the best combos, healing mode mechanics, fighting styles, and PvP strategies.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/3b82f6/fff?text=Crazy+Diamond+Guide",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Bizarre Lineage Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Bizarre lineage crazy diamond** is a versatile hybrid Stand specializing in high-impact melee combat and team-wide restoration.
- **Obtaining Method**: Acquired via **Stand Arrow** with a relatively high drop rate, making it an excellent choice for beginners.
- **Core Mechanic**: Features a unique **Heal Mode** (V-key) that swaps offensive pressure for the game's most efficient healing.
- **Best Style**: Pair with **Boxing** for maximum combo potential or **Hamon** for increased damage output against Vampires.
- **PvP Tier**: Currently sits in the **A-Tier** due to its incredible guard-breaking capabilities and "Bearing Shot" grab mechanics.
</Callout>

In the evolving landscape of Roblox's JoJo-inspired RPGs, few Stands offer the utility and raw power found in **bizarre lineage crazy diamond**. Inspired by Josuke Higashikata's iconic spirit, this Stand bridges the gap between a frontline bruiser and a dedicated support. Whether you are looking to dominate the 1v1 Colosseum or sustain your teammates during the grueling Yoshikage Kira raid, mastering Crazy Diamond is essential for any serious player in 2026. This comprehensive guide will break down every aspect of the Stand, from its fundamental moveset to advanced "Bearing Shot" grab combos that can bypass even the tightest defenses. We will also explore the optimal stat distributions and fighting style synergies to ensure you maximize your potential in both PvE and competitive PvP environments.

<Section icon={Map} title="Overview and Obtaining Crazy Diamond">

Before diving into the complex mechanics of **bizarre lineage crazy diamond**, you must first understand its place in the game's rarity hierarchy. Unlike Mythical Stands such as Made In Heaven or Whitesnake, Crazy Diamond is classified as an **Uncommon/Rare** Stand, making it highly accessible for players who have just started their journey in Morioh-cho.

**Video Highlights:**
- Detailed showcase of the basic M1 and M2 attack animations.
- Demonstration of the "Heal Mode" transition and its visual cues.
- Comparison between standard damage output and restorative capabilities.
- Overview of the "Bearing Shot" projectile and its hidden grab mechanic.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="X9JWWbF5tNg" title="Bizarre Lineage Crazy Diamond Showcase" />

To obtain this Stand, you must use a **Stand Arrow**, which can be found via the Meteor Shower world events at the Morioh Train Station or purchased from the Stand Arrow Merchant. Because it doesn't require the extreme luck of a Legendary pull, it serves as the perfect "bridge" Stand for players farming for their endgame builds.

| Feature | Detail |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Rarity** | Uncommon / Rare |
| **Source** | Stand Arrow / Lucky Arrow |
| **Primary Role** | Brawler / Support Healer |
| **Difficulty** | Beginner Friendly |
| **Best Matchup** | Killer Queen (Counters explosions with healing) |
| **Worst Matchup** | King Crimson (Time Erase disrupts combos) |

<Callout type="tip" title="Pro Tip">
If you are struggling to find Stand Arrows, wait for the **Meteor Shower** event which occurs every 2-3 hours. It is the most efficient way to stockpile arrows for rerolling your Stand.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Complete Moveset & Ability Breakdown">

The strength of **bizarre lineage crazy diamond** lies in its dual-mode functionality. By default, the Stand is in **Damage Mode**, focusing on high-speed barrages and crowd control. However, pressing the **V-key** toggles **Heal Mode**, which completely alters your interaction with other players.

### Damage Mode Abilities

In Damage Mode, you are a combo machine. The Stand utilizes heavy physical strikes to overwhelm opponents and break their guard.

| Key | Move Name | Description | Damage Type |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **E** | Dora Barrage | A rapid-fire punch sequence that deals consistent DPS. | Melee / Multi-hit |
| **R** | Bearing Shot | Fires a high-velocity projectile. Up close, it acts as a **Grab**. | Ranged / Grab |
| **Z** | Rock Trap | Traps the enemy in a stone pillar, stunning them for combos. | CC / Stun |
| **M2** | Heavy Punch | A powerful single strike that often results in a knockback. | Guard Break |
| **C** | Pulverizer | An instant-hit slam that continues the combo chain. | Melee |

### The "Bearing Shot" Secret
Most players treat Bearing Shot as a simple ranged poke. However, experienced users of **bizarre lineage crazy diamond** know that if you use this move while standing directly next to an opponent, it triggers a **Grab animation**. This grab not only deals significant damage but also resets the cooldown on certain follow-up attacks, allowing for "stupidly long" combo strings that can take an enemy from 100% to 0% health if they miss their dodge.

<Callout type="warning" title="Combat Warning">
While in **Heal Mode**, you cannot deal damage to enemies. Always ensure you have a clear opening before switching modes, as you will be vulnerable to attack during the transition animation.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Heart} title="Mastering the Restoration (Heal Mode)">

One of the most unique aspects of **bizarre lineage crazy diamond** is its ability to heal allies (and even NPCs) with extreme efficiency. When you activate **Heal Mode**, your Stand takes on a shimmering aura, and your attacks no longer hurt; they mend.

**Healing Effectiveness Factors:**
- **M1 Attacks**: Each light punch restores a small amount of health.
- **Barrage (E)**: Rapidly heals the target, perfect for saving an ally in a raid.
- **Heavy (M2)**: Provides a massive burst of healing (approx. 20+ HP depending on perks).
- **Wall Smash**: Instead of a damaging smash, you can create a protective barrier to block incoming projectiles.

### Optimal Healing Perks
To truly excel as a support, you should look for specific perks that enhance your restorative output. Perks like **Healing Blow** and **Good Friend** are essential, as they increase the percentage of health restored per hit.

| Perk Name | Effect | Synergy Level |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Healing Blow** | Increases heal amount on M1s and M2s. | ★★★★★ |
| **Good Friend** | Boosts healing effectiveness for party members. | ★★★★☆ |
| **No Witnesses** | Grants a 15% buff after defeating an enemy. | ★★★☆☆ |
| **Eraser** | Automatically executes enemies at 5% health. | ★★★★☆ |

<Callout type="success" title="Support Strategy">
In group Raids like the Yoshikage Kira battle, stay behind your tank (usually a Star Platinum or King Crimson user) and spam your Barrage in Heal Mode. This allows your team to ignore bomb damage and focus purely on DPS.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Best Builds and Fighting Style Synergies">

To maximize the potential of **bizarre lineage crazy diamond**, you must pair it with a complementary Fighting Style and Sub-Ability. Since Crazy Diamond relies on close-quarters combat and "sticking" to the enemy, styles that enhance your M1 speed and provide extra stuns are preferred.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="The Boxing Brawler">
    - **Style**: Boxing
    - **Sub-Ability**: Hamon
    - **Focus**: High DPS and fast M1s.
    - **Pros**: Incredible combo extension with Liver Crusher.
  </Card>
  <Card title="The Ultimate Tank">
    - **Style**: Karate
    - **Sub-Ability**: Vampire
    - **Focus**: Survivability and sustain.
    - **Pros**: Life steal combined with Heal Mode makes you unkillable.
  </Card>
  <Card title="The Technical Spacing">
    - **Style**: Kendo
    - **Sub-Ability**: Spin
    - **Focus**: Range and poking.
    - **Pros**: Uses Bearing Shot and Steel Balls to zone enemies.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

### Recommended Stat Allocation
For a balanced PvP build, you want to ensure you have enough health to survive burst combos while maintaining high Stand Power for your Dora Barrage.

| Attribute | Points | Reasoning |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Vitality** | 40 | Essential for surviving high-tier Stand combos. |
| **Stand Power** | 45 | Maximizes the damage of your Bearing Shot and Barrage. |
| **Durability** | 10 | Provides enough damage reduction for trade-offs. |
| **Speed** | 5 | Minor boost to help with chasing kiting opponents. |

<Callout type="info" title="Stat Tip">
Avoid dumping too many points into Health early on. In Bizarre Lineage, you only get roughly 3.3 HP per point, so focusing on **Stand Power** to end fights faster is often more efficient for leveling.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Advanced Combo Guide & Awakening">

Mastering the "One-Shot" combo with **bizarre lineage crazy diamond** requires precise timing and knowledge of the "Rock Trap" stun duration. Follow these steps to execute the most common high-damage chain.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="The Initiation">
    Start with 3 M1 attacks followed by a **Rock Trap (Z)**. This will stun the enemy in place, preventing them from dodging the next move.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="The Grab Extension">
    Quickly move close and use **Bearing Shot (R)**. If you are close enough, this triggers the grab animation, dealing burst damage and resetting your positioning.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="The Barrage Finisher">
    While the enemy is recovering from the grab, unleash your **Dora Barrage (E)**. If they try to block, use your **Heavy Punch (M2)** to break their guard.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="The Pulverizer Reset">
    End the combo with **Pulverizer (C)** to knock them back, giving you time to either switch to Heal Mode to recover or prepare for the next engagement.
  </Step>
</Steps>

### Stand Awakening Requirements
To unlock the true potential of Crazy Diamond, you must pursue **Awakening**. This enhances all your moves and provides a massive damage boost.

<Checklist
  id="cd-awakening"
  title="Awakening Requirements:"
  items={[
    "Reach 100+ Conjuring by using your Stand in combat",
    "Participate in at least 5 successful Raids",
    "Defeat the World Boss DIO to obtain rare materials",
    "Equip a Cosmetic that boosts Stand Power (e.g., Jotaro's Hat)"
  ]}
/>

<Callout type="tip" title="Farming Conjuring">
The fastest way to build Conjuring is to fight NPCs in the **Devil's Palm Desert**. Their high HP pools allow you to land more hits per encounter, accelerating your progress toward 100 Conjuring.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "Is Bizarre Lineage Crazy Diamond good for solo players?",
    answer: "Yes, it is one of the best solo Stands because you can fight enemies in Damage Mode and then switch to Heal Mode to recover your HP between fights without using items."
  },
  {
    question: "Can Crazy Diamond heal the user?",
    answer: "In the current 2026 build, Crazy Diamond primarily heals others. However, certain sub-abilities like Vampire or specific life-steal perks can provide self-sustain."
  },
  {
    question: "What is the best counter to Crazy Diamond?",
    answer: "King Crimson and The World are difficult matchups. Their ability to erase or stop time can interrupt your 'Bearing Shot' grab and leave you vulnerable."
  },
  {
    question: "How do I trigger the Bearing Shot grab consistently?",
    answer: "You must be within 'M1 range' of the target. If there is any significant gap between you and the enemy, it will fire as a projectile instead of a grab."
  }
]} />

</Section>
